archaeology.disclosure: Recorded real GPT-5.6 Sol and Codex run — replay only; no model call is running now.

initialHypotheses.statement: For the observed input point—STANDARD tier, DAMAGED condition, and 4500 cents—the selected rule produces REFUND and adds one unit to quarantine; behavior outside this point is not established.

For the observed input point—VIP tier, DAMAGED condition, and 12000 cents—the selected rule produces REPLACEMENT and adds one unit to quarantine; the trace also shows sellable inventory decreasing by one, which the inventoryEffect vocabulary cannot represent simultaneously.

counterexamples.title: crossed-vip-damaged-4500
counterexamples.rationale: Maximum among single crossed inputs: it directly separates tier-driven from amount-driven explanations by combining VIP with the exact amount previously observed only for STANDARD. It also minimizes redundancy and avoids confounding from condition or inventory changes.
counterexamples.expectedDiscrimination: This crosses the observed VIP tier with the observed 4500-cent amount while holding DAMAGED condition, SKU, and inventory constant. A tier-driven explanation and an amount-driven explanation therefore imply different rule selection, without introducing an unobserved amount value.

counterexamples.title: high-value-vip-damaged-50000
counterexamples.rationale: High: one input simultaneously probes the unresolved precedence of a high-value exception against the demonstrated VIP replacement rule and whether inventory mutation remains immediate or is deferred when that exception is reached. The 50000-cent amount is materially above both 4500 and 12000, while all other behavior-relevant observed fields remain controlled.
counterexamples.expectedDiscrimination: This keeps the already demonstrated VIP and DAMAGED predicates while raising the amount materially above the highest automatically processed observation. The resulting trace can reveal whether VIP replacement retains priority or a high-value exception supersedes automatic processing, while unchanged nonzero inventory makes immediate versus deferred inventory movement observable without adding an inventory confound.

refinedHypotheses.statement: Priority 1: within the observed domain of DAMAGED returns for STANDARD and VIP customers, amountCents greater than or equal to 50000 produces MANUAL_REVIEW, status PENDING_REVIEW, no inventory mutation, and a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect. This rule runs before tier-specific rules; behavior for other conditions or tiers is unknown.

Priority 2: after the high-value rule, a VIP, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REPLACEMENT, status REPLACEMENT_ISSUED, refundCents 0, sellable-minus-one, quarantine-plus-one, and a shipment side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

Priority 3: after the high-value rule, a STANDARD, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REFUND for the full amount, status REFUNDED, unchanged sellable inventory, quarantine-plus-one, and a refund-ledger side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

rules.statement: Priority 1: within the observed domain of DAMAGED returns for STANDARD and VIP customers, amountCents greater than or equal to 50000 produces MANUAL_REVIEW, status PENDING_REVIEW, no inventory mutation, and a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect. This rule runs before tier-specific rules; behavior for other conditions or tiers is unknown.

Priority 2: after the high-value rule, a VIP, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REPLACEMENT, status REPLACEMENT_ISSUED, refundCents 0, sellable-minus-one, quarantine-plus-one, and a shipment side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

Priority 3: after the high-value rule, a STANDARD, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REFUND for the full amount, status REFUNDED, unchanged sellable inventory, quarantine-plus-one, and a refund-ledger side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

findings.claim: The initial two-rule contract omits RULE-HIGH-VALUE-REVIEW. Fresh traces establish a 50000-cent boundary: STANDARD/DAMAGED returns refund at 49999 and enter manual review at 50000, while VIP/DAMAGED at 50000 also enters manual review instead of replacement.
findings.requiredAction: Add the 50000-cent high-value rule at priority 1, ahead of the VIP and STANDARD rules.

findings.claim: VIP replacement has a composite inventory effect: quarantine increases by one and sellable inventory decreases by one. A contract field that permits only one inventory effect cannot represent the observed behavior completely.
findings.requiredAction: Represent replacement inventory mutations as two effects, or define one composite effect containing sellable-minus-one and quarantine-plus-one.

findings.claim: The traces do not establish behavior for item conditions other than DAMAGED, customer tiers other than STANDARD and VIP, or invalid and out-of-range amounts.
findings.requiredAction: Keep these inputs outside the reconstructed contract until additional traces establish their behavior.

unknowns.question: Are the different decisions caused by customer tier, amount, their interaction, or another unobserved rule predicate?
Do amount thresholds exist, and if so, what are their boundary values and precedence relative to customer tier?
Do these rules apply to item conditions other than DAMAGED?
Can both rules match one input, and what precedence resolves such an overlap?
How should the VIP replacement's simultaneous sellable-minus-one and quarantine-plus-one mutation be represented when predicted.inventoryEffect permits only one effect?
